topic.name: Maritime Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to maritime operations, coastal management, and the regulation of vessels in support of marine competitions and commercial activities.

Document 1: America's Cup Act of 2011 - Authorizes vessels operating in preparation for, or in connection with, the 34th America's Cup competition.
Subjects any vessel participating in the 34th America's Cup as a competing or supporting vessel that has not received an eligibility certification to specified penalties.
Document 2: Amends the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to establish a Working Waterfront Grant Program.
to preserve and expand access to coastal waters for persons engaged in commercial fishing, recreational fishing, or other water-dependent coastal-related business.
Document 3: Allows taxpayers to elect to include any payment from BP PLC for damages related to the blowout and explosion on the mobile offshore drilling unit Deepwater Horizon.
in gross income ratably over a three-year period for tax purposes.

topic.name: Youth Development Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing opportunities and support for youth through mentoring, financial literacy education, and employment initiatives.

Document 1: Foster Care Mentoring Act of 2009
provide mentoring for children in foster care
Document 2: Young Adults Financial Literacy Act
effective programs in financial literacy education for young adults and families ages 15-24 years old
Document 3: Employ Young Americans Now Act
provide summer and year-round employment opportunities to low-income youth

topic.name: Offshore Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation regarding the exploration, development, and production of oil and natural gas in offshore areas of the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the availability of any area of the OCS off the Virginia coast for leasing for exploration for, or the development or production of, oil or natural gas unless the Governor of Virginia, with the concurrence of the Virginia legislature, has requested the Secretary of the Interior to make such area available.

Requires the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary action to ensure that oil and gas exploration, development, and production activities under federal leases of areas off the Virginia coast will result in no significant adverse effect on fish and wildlife, their habitat, subsistence resources, and the environment.
Document 2: Prohibit the Department of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to repeal limitations on export of Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) oil or gas.
Directs the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce to grant licenses to export crude oil except in certain circumstances.

topic.name: U.S. Postal and Courthouse Designations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the designation and renaming of U.S. postal facilities and courthouses, as well as the restructuring of judicial circuits within the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 152 North 5th Street in Laramie, Wyoming, as the 'Gale W. McGee Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 2601 2nd Avenue North, Billings, Montana, as the 'James F. Battin United States Courthouse.'
Declares that the 'James F. Battin United States Courthouse' located at 315 North 26th Street, Billings, Montana, shall no longer be so named.
Document 3: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into: (1) the Ninth Circuit, composed of California, Guam, Hawaii, and Northern Mariana Islands; and (2) the Twelfth Circuit, composed of Alaska, Arizona, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington.

topic.name: Legal Protections for Speech and Privacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at safeguarding free speech, privacy rights, and the public's right to access information in legal contexts.

Document 1: Free Speech Protection Act of 2008 - Allows any U.S. person against whom a lawsuit for defamation is brought in a foreign country...
engaged in a scheme to suppress rights under the First Amendment to the Constitution
Document 2: requires the National Security Agency (NSA) to have access to business records...
amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005
Document 3: Sunshine in Litigation Act of 2009 - Amends the federal judicial code to prohibit a court from entering an order restricting the disclosure of information...
the public interest in the disclosure of potential health or safety hazards is outweighed by a specific and substantial interest in maintaining the confidentiality of the information

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative actions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs, including contract extensions, project authorizations, and specific provisions for rehabilitation services.

Document 1: Medicare Cost Contract Extension and Refinement Act of 2007
extend the period reasonable cost reimbursement contracts under Medicare
Document 2: continues the Medicaid emergency psychiatric demonstration project
authorizes the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services to replace with another state
Document 3: Gulf States Medicare Rehabilitation Unit Access Act of 2007
provides for certification of certain rehabilitation units of general acute care hospitals affected by Hurricane Katrina

topic.name: Unemployment and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures related to unemployment benefits and social security provisions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until May 31, 2014
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to disburse a $250 payment for a particular year to recipients of Social Security benefits
if no cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) is payable for any month in such a year
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and Title II of the Social Security Act to exempt from social security taxes remuneration paid to any elected or appointed member
of any political subdivision of a state if such remuneration is not more than $1,300

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at reforming and supporting higher education funding and opportunities for students.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the deduction for interest on education loans
extends the grace period before payment must begin on Federal Direct Stafford Loans
Document 2: makes Hispanic-serving institutions and Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander-serving institutions eligible for technical and financial assistance
establish preservation training and degree programs
Document 3: eliminates the program awarding Academic Competitiveness Grants
reauthorizes and appropriates funds for National Science and Mathematics Access to Retain Talent Grants

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and security in aviation through regulations and modernization efforts.

Document 1: Helicopter Fuel System Safety Act
ensure that certain fuel system crash resistance requirements are met
Document 2: modernizing the C-130 aircraft fleet
ensure that all C-130 aircraft are capable of meeting applicable regulations of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
Document 3: Arm All Pilots Act of 2015
revises requirements for the federal flight deck officer program

topic.name: Drug Court Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the establishment, funding, and regulation of drug courts and related programs for nonviolent offenders, particularly veterans.

Document 1: authorize appropriations for the drug courts program
modify certain program requirements relating to grant authority
Document 2: authorize the Attorney General to make grants to states
develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
Document 3: prohibit the Department of Justice from allocating funding to a state or local government
collect unpaid debt from an individual placed on probation

topic.name: Energy Efficiency and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing energy efficiency, promoting renewable energy, and implementing policies for sustainable energy practices.

Document 1: requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish a voluntary electric and thermal energy productivity challenge grant program
promote energy efficiency projects, encourage streamlining and automation
Document 2: impose an excise tax on the carbon content of any taxable fuel
Sets the amount of such tax at $10 per ton of the carbon dioxide produced
Document 3: require each state regulatory authority with ratemaking authority to conduct a hearing
Requires retail electric suppliers to make net metering available to retail customers

topic.name: Labor Relations Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation of labor relations, including collective bargaining rights and voting rights in labor contexts.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for determination by the National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) of an appropriate bargaining unit
Requires the NLRB, instead, to determine a unit as appropriate for collective bargaining if it consists of employees that share a sufficient community of interest.
Document 2: Excludes employees of the Internal Revenue Service from provisions of federal personnel law allowing federal employees to organize, bargain collectively, and participate in labor organizations.

topic.name: Health Care Workforce Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving health care services and workforce training for various populations, including veterans and individuals with mental health needs.

Document 1: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to require the Secretary of Labor to establish and carry out a health professions training demonstration project
train certain unemployed workers from the manufacturing or service sector for employment as health care professionals
Document 2: Amends the Department of Veterans Affairs Health Care Programs Enhancement Act of 2001
require a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to enter into chronic care improvement program operator agreements
provide for the development, testing, evaluation, and implementation of severe mental illness chronic care improvement demonstration programs

topic.name: Energy Policy and Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to energy resources and their management in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to authorize the Secretary of Energy to barter, transfer, or sell uranium to eligible entities at fair market value.
Requires the Secretary to maintain a sufficient inventory of uranium to meet current and foreseeable needs of the missions and programs of DOE.
Document 2: Amends the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 to exempt any action relating to the development, deployment, or operation of a solar energy project from environmental impact statement requirements.
Requires each federal agency to cease the preparation of an EIS to the extent that its preparation would delay or otherwise interfere with the action.

topic.name: Legislation on Harmful Activities
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at prohibiting and penalizing various harmful activities including drug distribution, child exploitation, and animal fighting.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem to be adulterated any unfinished dextromethorphan that is possessed, received, or distributed in violation of this Act.
Prohibits a person from possessing or receiving unfinished dextromethorphan unless the person is registered with the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a mandatory minimum prison term of two years for possession of child pornography.
Make it a crime to knowingly access with intent to view child pornography.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful to knowingly sponsor or exhibit an animal in an animal fighting venture.
Prohibits knowingly sponsoring or exhibiting a bird in a fighting venture in a state where it would not otherwise be a violation of the law.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumer rights and ensuring fair practices in financial and personal data transactions.

Document 1: expands the definition of 'consumer financial product or service' to include rent-to-own transactions
prevent unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices
Document 2: requires verification of an individual's employment authorization and identity
places the burden of resolving errors in the verification mechanism on the individual
Document 3: allows consent to be provided through electronic means using the Internet
requires the video tape service provider to provide an opportunity for the consumer to withdraw such consent

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Internal Revenue Code to simplify tax structures and provide incentives for economic activities.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 19 percent
Repeals: (1) the alternative minimum tax; (2) all income tax credits; (3) estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide tax incentives for investment in an Economic Freedom Zone
Exempts an area designated as an Economic Freedom Zone from compliance with specified environmental regulations.
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a full deduction in the current taxable year of the cost of replanting lost or damaged citrus plants.

topic.name: Environmental Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving environmental infrastructure and safety in the United States.

Document 1: Amends provisions concerning Appalachian regional development
to revise the definition of 'Appalachian region'
Document 2: amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish an integrated planning and permitting process
Document 3: Amends the National Dam Safety Program Act
to require the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to establish a program to provide grant assistance to states

topic.name: International Trade and Currency Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to currency exchange rates, trade regulations, and economic policies impacting international commerce.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make public and report biannually to Congress on international monetary policy and currency exchange rates
an analysis of currency market developments and the relationship between the U.S. dollar and the currencies of major economies
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States Trade Representative should encourage other countries to establish commercially meaningful de minimis values for express and postal shipments
Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to increase from $200 to $800 for duty-free imports into the United States

topic.name: Women and Minorities in STEM
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing the participation of women and underrepresented minorities in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics fields.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to develop a policy for federal science agencies to carry out a program of workshops that educate specified federally funded researchers about methods that minimize the effects of gender bias
Requires the support of at least one workshop every two years among the federal science agencies in the major science and engineering disciplines.
Document 3: This bill amends the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 to designate as Jeannette Rankin Women and Minorities in STEM Fields Program Grants
to increase participation by women and underrepresented minorities from rural areas in the fields of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM)

topic.name: Women's Rights and Health Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts aimed at promoting women's rights, health, and empowerment both domestically and internationally.

Document 1: protection of the rights of women and girls in Afghanistan
advance the rights of women throughout Afghanistan
Document 2: address the social and health issues that lead to obstetric fistula
support treatment of obstetric fistula
Document 3: promote better understanding of global perspectives
use independently produced documentary films to promote understanding

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to agricultural policies, including taxation, commodity payments, and promotional programs for agricultural products.

Document 1: Christmas Tree Tax Exemption Act requires the Department of Agriculture to permit 'choose and cut' Christmas tree producers to opt out of USDA's Christmas Tree Promotion.
The order was authorized by the Commodity Promotion, Research, and Information Act of 1996.
Document 2: Free Sugar Act of 2011 amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 to repeal the sugar loan program.
Document 3: Rural America Preservation Act of 2007 amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to reduce maximum annual direct and counter-cyclical commodity payments.
Establishes an annual combined limitation of $75,000 for such payments.

topic.name: Native American Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the recognition, empowerment, and rights of Native American tribes and individuals.

Document 1: Code Talkers Recognition Act of 2008
award of gold medals in recognition of the service of Native American code talkers
Document 2: Lumbee Act of June 7, 1956
federally recognized status of any Indian tribe
Document 3: American Indian Empowerment Act of 2013
transfer land the federal government holds in trust for a federally recognized Indian tribe

topic.name: Counterterrorism Strategies
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislative and strategic efforts to combat terrorism and its manifestations, particularly through social media and geopolitical assessments.

Document 1: requires the President to transmit to Congress a report, an evaluation, and a strategy on U.S. efforts to combat terrorists' and terrorist organizations' use of social media
an analysis of how terrorists and terrorist organizations are using social media
Document 2: report to the congressional defense committees on the U.S. Pacific Command's munitions strategy for the next ten years
evaluate options for short-range missile, rocket, and artillery defense capabilities
Document 3: directs the Secretary of State to designate the Pakistani Taliban as a foreign terrorist organization under the Immigration and Nationality Act

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Impact Analysis
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the accountability and analysis of regulatory actions by federal agencies, focusing on their economic impacts and compliance costs.

Document 1: require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis
Document 2: require the Director of the Congressional Budget Office to include estimates of the potential job creation or job loss
require each initial regulatory flexibility analysis to contain a detailed statement estimating the additional cumulative economic impact of the proposed rule on small businesses
Document 3: revise requirements for federal agency rulemaking
consider reasonable alternatives for a new rule

topic.name: Tax Policy and Administration
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of tax policy, including tax preparation services, remote sales tax collection, and identification requirements for tax credits.

Document 1: requires the Department of the Treasury to continue to operate the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Free File Program
provide free commercial-type online individual income tax preparation and electronic filing services to the lowest 70% of taxpayers by income
Document 2: authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement to require all sellers not qualifying for a small-seller exception to collect and remit sales and use taxes
extends the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access and on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the identification requirement for the child tax credit
require taxpayers to provide a valid identification number on their tax returns

topic.name: Government Funding and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to government funding mechanisms and oversight of appropriations for various purposes, including education and military engagement.

Document 1: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) to reauthorize and make appropriations for the grant program to assist states
Sets the appropriation for each fiscal year as an amount equal to the difference between the amount of such percentage that is applicable that fiscal year and a specified amount.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to establish in the Treasury the Religious Freedom Peace Tax Fund
Requires that amounts deposited in the Fund be allocated annually to any appropriation not for a military purpose.
Document 3: Prohibits the U.S. government from obligating or expending funds to increase the number of U.S. forces in Iraq above the number for such forces which existed as of January 9, 2007

topic.name: Health and Social Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to health care, social services, and welfare improvements.

Document 1: Medical FSA Improvement Act of 2011
allows amounts in a flexible spending arrangement (FSA) that are not spent for medical care to be distributed to the FSA participant as taxable income
Document 2: Partnership for Children and Families Act
revises the eligibility requirements for adoption assistance and foster care maintenance payments
Document 3: Health Care Waiver Fairness Act of 2011

requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish waiver processes under which the administrator of a health plan may seek to waive the application of a health insurance coverage requirement

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative acts aimed at improving access to healthcare services and education for various health issues.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to states and Indian tribes for colorectal cancer programs
developing and disseminating public information and education programs for the detection and control of colorectal cancer
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to increase the availability of treatment for abuse of heroin, cocaine, methamphetamine
establish programs to provide for and coordinate the provision of wrap-around services, such as medical services, job training services, and housing assistance
Document 3: Prohibits payment to a hospital under titles XVIII or XIX of the Social Security Act unless the hospital promptly provides information about emergency contraception
Requires the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to develop and disseminate information on emergency contraception

topic.name: Federal Program Improvement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of federal programs across various sectors, including agriculture, health, and administration.

Document 1: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security to identify career paths for agriculture specialists.
Develop a plan to improve agriculture specialist recruitment and retention.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to coordinate with the heads of federal agencies to eliminate, consolidate, or streamline federal programs.
Apply any savings towards deficit reduction.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a working group to develop best practices on access to prescription drug labeling for the visually impaired.
Conduct an informational and educational program to inform the public and pharmacists about such guidance and practices.

topic.name: U.S. Military Funding Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the allocation and restrictions of federal funds for military operations and detention policies.

Document 1: Prohibits federal funds from being expended, obligated, or used in any manner to support directly or indirectly operations in or over Libya
Requires rescission of any obligated, but not expended, funds to support such operations.
Document 2: Prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used to transfer an individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay
No U.S. agency funds may be used to fund activities, envoys, or offices to carry out activities to close Guantanamo naval station.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Defense (DOD) to post on the DOD public website, the cost to each U.S. taxpayer of each of the wars in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Syria.

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and technologies in various transportation sectors.

Document 1: requires each rail carrier to develop a plan for positive train control system
increases penalties for violations of rail safety regulations
Document 2: allows a tax credit for advanced safety systems in commercial vehicles
defines qualified commercial vehicle advanced safety system
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Defense to ensure safety standards for military facilities overseas
incorporates industry safety standards into military regulations

topic.name: Water Rights and Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, allocation, and enhancement of water resources for various projects and tribes in the United States.

Document 1: expands the current authorization for the construction or rehabilitation and operation and maintenance of conjunctive use wells
increasing from 2% to 4% the portion of funds that may be used for the survey, recovery, protection, preservation, and display of archaeological resources
Document 2: Declares that the Madera Water Supply Enhancement Project, California, is feasible
Requires all planning, design, and construction of the Project to be undertaken in accordance with a cooperative agreement between the Secretary of the Interior and the Madera Irrigation District
Document 3: allocates to the Tribe 50,000 acre-feet per year of stored water in Lake Sherburne Reservoir as part of the tribal water right
requires the Secretary to carry out such activities as are necessary relating to planning, design, and construction for rehabilitation and improvement of the Blackfeet Irrigation Project

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative efforts aimed at regulating air quality and greenhouse gas emissions to protect the environment and public health.

Document 1: directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to permit the distribution, sale, and consumption in the United States of remaining inventories of CFC epinephrine inhalers
not take any enforcement action or otherwise seek to restrict the distribution, sale, or consumption of such inhalers
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act (CAA) to require the Administrator of the Environment Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a sulfur dioxide allowance trading program
Directs the Administrator to issue allowances authorizing specified annual tonnage amounts of emissions of sulfur dioxide
Document 3: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to determine and publish the quantity of each greenhouse gas (GHG)
Directs each covered entity to report to the Administrator its GHG emissions for the prior calendar year

topic.name: Gender Equality Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing wage discrimination and defining marriage in the context of state recognition.

Document 1: Amends the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 2: Amends the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 3: Prohibits the term 'marriage' from including any relationship that the state, territory, or possession does not recognize as a marriage.
Prohibits the term 'spouse' from including an individual who is a party to a relationship that is not recognized as a marriage by that state, territory, or possession.

topic.name: Immigration Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative changes related to immigration, including employment-based visas, educational benefits for undocumented immigrants, and passport regulations for individuals associated with foreign terrorist organizations.

Document 1: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 10% of the worldwide employment-based immigration level available to qualified immigrants
the computation of the total number of immigrant visas that may be made available to natives of any single foreign state or dependent area in a fiscal year is revised.
Document 2: prohibit unlawful aliens from being eligible for any postsecondary education benefit unless every citizen and national of this country is eligible
allows any citizen or national who is enrolled at a postsecondary educational institution that is alleged to have violated that prohibition to bring a civil action.

topic.name: Military Service Benefits and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and regulations related to military service appointments, benefits for surviving spouses and children, and provisions affecting military personnel and their families.

Document 3: Amends federal employment, armed forces, military pay, and veterans' benefits law
authorizes the provision of the following benefits to a member of the Armed Forces who is discharged at the member's request

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on digital-to-analog converter boxes.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain compression-ignition internal combustion piston engines.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.

topic.name: Military Construction Appropriations
topic.description: Funding and appropriations for military construction and related activities for the Department of Defense.

Document 1: Appropriates funds for FY2009 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force
Directs the Secretary of Defense to notify the appropriate congressional committees 30 days in advance of the plans and scope of any proposed military exercise involving U.S. personnel
Document 2: Appropriates funds for FY2009 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force

Directs the Secretary to report to the appropriations committees on actions taken by DOD and the Department of State during the previous fiscal year to encourage other member nations of NATO, Japan, South Korea, and U.S. allies bordering the Arabian Sea to assume a greater share of the common defense burden

topic.name: Federal Spectrum Management
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the allocation and use of electromagnetic spectrum for federal and commercial purposes.

Document 1: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to allow federal entities that utilize government station licenses to participate in the incentive auction program.
Establishes in the Treasury a Federal Spectrum Incentive Fund to be administered by the Office of Management and Budget.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring warning labels on baby monitors to alert users that video and audio from such devices may be intercepted.
Regulations pertain to the management of communication devices and their spectrum use.
Document 3: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to provide additional unlicensed spectrum in the 5850-5925 megahertz band.
Sets forth a process for the FCC to test mitigation measures and methods of sharing spectrum with unlicensed devices.

topic.name: Political Reform Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming lobbying practices and electoral processes in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code regarding the two-year ban on lobbying contacts by former Senators
Imposes a five-year lobbying ban on all former Members of Congress
Document 2: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives
Modifies the formula regarding the number of presidential electors
Document 3: Eliminate the limitations on direct contributions from individuals and political committees to candidates
Require 24-hour notification for all direct contributions to candidates in excess of $200

topic.name: Education Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving educational access, financial support, and literacy for students in various educational settings.

Document 1: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
allow states to allocate school improvement grant funds
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
deem a student independent for financial aid purposes if such student has been verified as both a homeless child or youth
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education of 1965
authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states to integrate financial literacy education

topic.name: U.S. Land Management and Preservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, preservation, and designation of significant land areas in the United States, including national parks and wilderness areas.

Document 1: conduct a special resource study of the national significance, suitability, and feasibility of including the Honouliuli Gulch in the National Park System
significance as related to the forcible internment of Japanese Americans
Document 2: amends the FLAME Act to revise how the FLAME Funds are funded
authorize USDA to conduct projects that involve the management or sale of national forest material
Document 3: designates certain lands in the Coronado National Forest as wilderness
to be known as the 'Tumacacori Highlands Wilderness.'

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions, insurance companies, and accounting practices to ensure transparency and limit risk in the financial system.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Makes certain technical and conforming amendments to the Banking Act of 1933 to limit the business of national banks.
Document 2: Defines 'captive insurance company' as an insurance company wholly owned directly or indirectly by a single parent company.
Excludes a captive insurance company from the meaning of a 'nonadmitted insurer' and from any requirements applying to a nonadmitted insurer.
Document 3: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to make Public Company Accounting Oversight Board disciplinary proceedings public.

topic.name: Border and Transportation Security
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing security measures for cross-border rail and air transportation in the United States.

Document 1: directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to report to Congress on the number of high-risk rail shipments annually entering the United States
an assessment of whether additional radiation detection equipment is necessary
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to calculate the annual fuel expense cost for all United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement vehicles
Document 3: revises minimum requirements for the deployment of armed law enforcement personnel at security screening locations at certain airports
ensuring airport security and cooperation between all relevant parties

topic.name: Labeling Regulations in Consumer Products
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the transparency and accuracy of labeling for various consumer products, including food, medical devices, and personal care items.

Document 1: Gluten in Medicine Disclosure Act of 2013
requires labeling of ingredients derived from grains or starches
Document 2: Accurate Labeling of Menstrual Products Act of 2016
requires menstrual products to include a list of ingredients on the label
Document 3: requires warning labels on video games
linked to aggressive behavior

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Safety and Manufacturing
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative acts focused on safety education and domestic manufacturing standards.

Document 1: Prohibits heads of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), the Department of Defense (DOD), and the Department of Transportation (DOT) from obligating or expending funds... unless all of the iron and steel used in such project is produced in the United States.
Makes any person who intentionally labels or represents a product used in a project under this Act as 'Made in America' when it is not ineligible for another department contract from for six years.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to award grants... for initiating, expanding, or improving fire safety education programs.
Requires an institution to use grant funds to initiate, expand, or improve a fire safety education program that... ensures maximum exposure to and increased awareness of... fire safety by students.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing support, funding, and opportunities for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to temporarily extend the 100% exclusion from gross income of gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock

authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to guarantee the payment of up to $4 billion per fiscal year for debentures or participating securities issued by small business investment companies
Document 2: amends the Small Business Act to revise requirements for increasing prime federal contracting opportunities for small business concerns
SBA shall include in such annual reports awards to these small business concerns that were purchased by another entity after the initial contract was awarded
Document 3: allow certain small businesses a refundable tax credit for 15% of the first-year wages paid to new employees
expands the authority of the Administrator to guarantee small business loans

topic.name: Housing and Mortgage Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at facilitating home buying and refinancing processes, particularly for individuals and veterans.

Document 1: one-time tax credit for 15% of the purchase price
requires repayment if the taxpayer sells the residence
Document 2: extends VA demonstration projects on guaranteeing veterans' mortgage loans
Document 3: refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages
prohibition on borrower fees

topic.name: Land Exchange Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to land exchanges and management involving federal and state lands.

Document 1: Extinguishes all claims and all right, title, and interest in specified Indian and non-Indian land as part of the settlement of disputes within the Fort Hall Indian Reservation.
Requires the non-Indian land to be held in trust by the United States for the Tribes.
Document 2: This bill declares that, if the state of Alaska offers to convey to the Department of the Interior 43,093 acres of state-owned land, Interior shall convey to Alaska, in exchange, 206 acres of federal land.
The values of the federal and nonfederal lands to be exchanged shall be equal.
Document 3: Amends the Military Lands Withdrawal Act of 1999 to declare that the withdrawal and reservation of certain lands and interests... shall not terminate except by an election.
Currently the withdrawal and reservation of such lands must terminate, except in certain circumstances, 25 years after November 6, 2001.
